Phase-25.NAME: Session Security Hardening
Phase-25.goal: Harden user and admin session handling after UI flows are simplified, with rollback-aware migration away from fragile browser token storage.

scope.included: User/admin auth smoke tests, refresh behavior, logout, role gates, browser storage checks, and compatibility rollback.
scope.excluded: Deploy/install script changes, password-based server deployment changes, and unrelated UI redesign work.

Phase-25.step-1: Audit current auth responses, cookies, bearer token compatibility, refresh flow, logout behavior, and admin role gates.
Phase-25.step-2: Plan user frontend session migration with rollback and explicit tests for login, register, refresh, logout, and 401 retry.
Phase-25.step-3: Plan admin frontend session migration with rollback and explicit tests for admin login, refresh, logout, and role rejection.
Phase-25.step-4: Verify token blacklist expectations and graceful degradation when Redis is unavailable.
Phase-25.step-5: Add browser storage and auth smoke gates before any session storage behavior changes are merged.

verification.check-3: Browser storage check confirms expected token/session behavior.
verification.check-4: Rollback path is documented before execution.

outcome.result-1: Created docs/security/SESSION-PHASE-25.xml with auth audit, migration order, rollback path, and operator-required gates.
outcome.result-2: Added scripts/session-security-smoke.mjs to guard current cookies, browser storage, refresh, logout, and blacklist expectations.
outcome.result-3: Fixed admin frontend refresh compatibility by persisting admin_refresh_token at login and clearing it on logout/setToken(null).

residual-risk.risk-1: Cookie-only session migration is intentionally not executed in this phase because it changes live auth behavior and needs an HTTPS or production-like smoke target.
residual-risk.risk-2: Current user and admin frontends still use rollback-compatible localStorage token storage until cookie-auth migration is explicitly approved.
